text.title: Impl Plan 100k Rule Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding Impl Plan 100k Rule. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: Every TASK_*.md front-matter records est_tokens ≤ 100000; tasks above the cap are split before execution.
rule.rationale: 100k aligns with reliable Sonnet/Opus working set; above that, compaction loss is significant.

rule.statement: Estimate = input_tokens + (tool_calls × avg_tool_response) + output_tokens; the formula is recorded once and applied uniformly.
rule.rationale: Without a formula, estimates drift across authors.

rule.statement: Oversized tasks are split into smaller atomic units; never silently shrunk by removing scope.
rule.rationale: Shrinking masks the cost; splitting preserves total work visibility.

rule.statement: Tasks without est_tokens front-matter are rejected by the validator.
rule.rationale: Missing estimates leak under deadline pressure.

rule.statement: If actual tokens exceed estimate by >30%, the task is logged and the formula is reviewed in the next grooming pass.
rule.rationale: Drift signals formula-bug or scope-creep; track it explicitly.

rule.statement: Deep-research tasks may carry est_tokens 200k with explicit reason 'deep-research'; no other exceptions allowed.
rule.rationale: Some research tasks legitimately need a wider window; force a documented reason.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-impl-plan-100k-rule.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
